headline: Russian and Chinese state hackers fielded new espionage backdoors as compromised third-party vendors drained $3 million from Polymarket and widened Europe's ransomware toll.

emerging_trends.text: Russia's Turla and a Chinese-speaking crew both surfaced fresh custom implants this week, with STOCKSTAY striking Ukrainian government and military networks and SharkLoader dropping Cobalt Strike on Asian governments, a reminder that state espionage keeps out-engineering signature-based defenses.

Compromised third-party vendors funneled fresh damage in every direction—a malicious script that drained $3 million from Polymarket users, two dozen more Klue-Salesforce victims, and a Black Kite finding that suppliers now anchor Europe's ransomware surge—showing supply-chain trust remains the cheapest way in.
emerging_trends.topic_title: Polymarket and Klue: Third-Party Breaches Multiply

emerging_trends.text: AI development tooling is becoming its own attack surface, as a flaw in Amazon Q Developer let a booby-trapped repository steal a developer's cloud credentials through Model Context Protocol server configs.
emerging_trends.topic_title: Amazon Q Developer Flaw Exposes Cloud Credentials

topics.title: State-Sponsored Backdoors: SharkLoader and StockStay
topics.latest_developments: Kaspersky exposed SharkLoader, a previously undocumented dropper deploying Cobalt Strike Beacon in a campaign it calls StrikeShark against a diplomatic organization in Indonesia and government bodies in Taiwan, while Google's Threat Intelligence Group detailed STOCKSTAY, a new .NET backdoor Russia's Turla aims at Ukrainian government and military networks and parties tracking Italian foreign policy.
topics.summary: SharkLoader hands operators a Cobalt Strike foothold, and STOCKSTAY gives Turla persistent .NET access for long-term espionage. Government, diplomatic, and software-development targets across Asia and Ukraine should hunt for both.

sources.title: New SharkLoader Malware Deploys Cobalt Strike in StrikeShark Cyberattacks
Mystery hackers use novel SharkLoader dropper against governments, software devs
Google Details Turla's New STOCKSTAY Backdoor Used in Ukraine Espionage Attacks
Turla group adds more malware to Russia's espionage efforts against Ukraine

topics.title: Amazon Q Developer Flaw Exposes Cloud Credentials
topics.latest_developments: AWS patched CVE-2026-12957, a high-severity flaw in Amazon Q Developer that let a malicious repository abuse Model Context Protocol server configs to run commands and steal a developer's cloud credentials the moment they trusted the workspace, as SecurityWeek flagged a broader MCP overhaul that shifts security duties onto developers and platform operators.
topics.summary: Amazon Q Developer is AWS's AI coding assistant; opening a poisoned repository and trusting the workspace handed attackers the developer's AWS keys. AWS has patched the flaw and urges customers to update.

sources.title: Amazon Q Flaw Enabled Cloud Credential Theft via Malicious Repositories
Amazon Q Developer Flaw Could Let Malicious Repos Run Code via MCP Configs
New Enterprise-Ready MCP Specification Brings New Security Challenges

topics.title: Polymarket and Klue: Third-Party Breaches Multiply
topics.latest_developments: Polymarket pledged to reimburse customers who lost about $3 million after attackers breached a third-party vendor and injected a malicious script into the prediction market's web frontend, while roughly two dozen more companies notified customers of the Klue-Salesforce breach and the hackers themselves got hacked.
topics.summary: Attackers reached Polymarket through a third-party vendor and slipped a script into its frontend to steal user funds, which the firm will reimburse. The parallel Klue-Salesforce breach keeps widening its victim list.

sources.title: Polymarket customers lose $3 million in supply-chain attack
$3 Million Reportedly Stolen in Polymarket Hack
More Klue Breach Victims Identified as Hackers Get Hacked

topics.title: PTC Windchill Flaw Exploited in the Wild
topics.latest_developments: CISA added CVE-2026-12569, a critical remote-code-execution flaw in PTC Windchill PDMlink and FlexPLM software, to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog on June 26 after spotting ongoing web-shell attacks, the first confirmed exploitation in the wild.
topics.summary: PTC Windchill manages product designs and engineering data for manufacturers, and the bug grants remote code execution on those servers. Operators should apply PTC's fix and inspect for web shells.

sources.title: CISA Adds Exploited PTC Windchill RCE Flaw to KEV as Web Shell Attacks Continue
First-Ever Exploitation of PTC Windchill Vulnerability Discovered in the Wild

topics.title: Quantum Deadline Strains Budgets
topics.latest_developments: Dark Reading detailed how meeting President Trump's 2030 post-quantum migration deadline will strain budgets across multivendor IT and OT environments plagued by visibility gaps and misaligned update cycles, as ZeroTier shipped release candidate 2 of ZeroTier Quantum, pushing its CNSA 2.0-compliant quantum-secure networking toward general availability.
topics.summary: An executive order sets a 2030 deadline to migrate federal systems to post-quantum cryptography, and interoperability gaps make compliance costly and complex. Vendors like ZeroTier are racing CNSA 2.0-ready products to market.

sources.title: Meeting Trump's 2030 Quantum Deadline Will be Expensive, Complex
ZeroTier Quantum RC2 brings post-quantum security closer to general availability

topics.title: European Ransomware Rides Third-Party Suppliers
topics.latest_developments: Black Kite's 2026 European Cyber Risk Report, drawn from 2,066 ransomware incidents across 31 countries between January 2025 and April 2026, found attacks on European organizations climbing in early 2026 with third-party suppliers now a leading point of entry.
topics.summary: The report ties Europe's rising ransomware toll to weak vendors in the supply chain. Organizations should vet, segment, and monitor their third-party suppliers as a primary defense.

sources.title: Ransomware gangs find Europe's weakest link in third-party suppliers

weather: Tonight: Cloudy then Slight Chance Showers And Thunderstorms, low 64F.
Saturday Night: Showers And Thunderstorms Likely then Slight Chance Showers And Thunderstorms, low 65F.

games.recap: Brandon Lowe, Henry Davis each homer to lead Pirates over Mariners 5-1

games.result: Reds 4 · Pirates 4 · Top 8th (in progress at last update)

business_politics.title: U.S. Strikes Iran, Threatening the Ceasefire
business_politics.latest_developments: The United States launched fresh military strikes on Iran on June 26, escalating past President Trump's earlier verbal blame for the cargo-ship attack a day before.
business_politics.summary: Washington hit Iran after a drone attack damaged a commercial vessel in the Strait of Hormuz, calling Tehran's move unwarranted aggression against commercial shipping and a breach of the framework ceasefire between the two adversaries; U.S. oil futures climbed back above $70 a barrel in late trading.

sources.title: US launches strikes on Iran after Tehran’s attack on container ship
U.S. Launches Fresh Attacks on Iran

business.title: Schwebel's Sets July 4 Shutdown
business.latest_developments: KDKA reported June 26 that Schwebel Baking Company will begin liquidating and close for good starting July 4, putting a firm date on the previously announced wind-down.
business.summary: The 120-year-old bread maker Schwebel Baking Company will end production and lay off workers across western Pennsylvania as it pursues a formal liquidation, shuttering a staple regional brand.

sources.title: Schwebel Baking Company to shut down after 120 years in business

business.title: Allegheny County May Drop Spouses From Health Plan
business.latest_developments: WPXI's 11 Investigates reported June 26 that Allegheny County employees are just learning their spouses could be removed from the county's medical insurance plan.
business.summary: Allegheny County is weighing a major change to worker health benefits that would push employee spouses off the county insurance plan, a cost-cutting move affecting thousands of county workers and their families.

sources.title: 11 Investigates Exclusive: County employees face major change to health care benefits

around_town.title: Code Red Heat Advisory, Cooling Centers Next Week
around_town.latest_developments: Allegheny County issued a Code Red Heat Advisory for several days next week on June 26 and will open five Pittsburgh cooling centers from 8 a.m. to 7 p.m.
around_town.summary: A heat wave will push temperatures high across Allegheny County early next week, prompting the county to open five cooling centers in Pittsburgh and warn residents, following a rainy start to the weekend.

sources.title: Allegheny County issues ‘Code Red Heat Advisory’ for several days next week
5 cooling centers will open in Pittsburgh next week amid high temperatures

around_town.title: Commercial Street Closed a Second Day for Bridge Testing
around_town.latest_developments: PennDOT closed Commercial Street near Frick Park for a second straight day June 26 for added testing, without explaining why the extra day was needed.
around_town.summary: PennDOT shut Commercial Street to test the system that will move a new bridge span into place near Frick Park, extending the closure beyond the single day originally scheduled.

sources.title: Commercial Street closed for second day as PennDOT crews do more testing

around_town.title: Shapiro Pushes Data-Center Incentives as His Party Wants More
around_town.latest_developments: The Post-Gazette reported June 26 that Governor Josh Shapiro favors incentives to make data centers behave, while fellow Pennsylvania Democrats want more aggressive limits.
around_town.summary: Governor Josh Shapiro is pitching incentives to shape data-center development in Pennsylvania, splitting with members of his own party in Harrisburg who passed bills letting municipalities impose moratoriums and curb the projects' tax breaks.

sources.title: Shapiro wants to use incentives to push data centers to behave better. His own party wants to be more aggressive.

events.title: Nelly, Third Eye Blind Headline America250 Concert at the Point
events.latest_developments: Pittsburgh and America250PA announced June 26 that Nelly and Third Eye Blind will headline the Commonwealth Concert at Point State Park on Saturday, June 27.
events.summary: The America250PA Commonwealth Concert Series comes to Point State Park in Downtown Pittsburgh on Saturday, June 27, headlined by Nelly and Third Eye Blind, with Pittsburgh native Frzy as special guest and former Steelers quarterback Charlie Batch hosting.

sources.title: Nelly and Third Eye Blind to headline America250PA Commonwealth Concert in Pittsburgh

around_teams.title: Why Chris Boswell Is Kicking Farther
around_teams.latest_developments: A Post-Gazette feature June 26 examined why Steelers kicker Chris Boswell and other NFL kickers are booting field goals farther than ever before.
around_teams.summary: The Post-Gazette dug into the league-wide surge in field-goal distance, using Steelers kicker Chris Boswell as a case study in the technique, equipment, and training behind today's record-long kicks.

sources.title: Why are Chris Boswell and other NFL kickers booting the football farther than ever before?

around_teams.title: Pirates Mailbag Tackles ABS Struggles
around_teams.latest_developments: A Post-Gazette mailbag June 26 asked how the Pirates can fix their abysmal success rate on automated ball-strike challenges, alongside All-Star voting and trade-deadline questions.
around_teams.summary: Beat writers fielded reader questions on the Pirates' poor record challenging calls under the automated ball-strike system, Paul Skenes's All-Star candidacy, and the looming MLB trade deadline.

sources.title: Pirates mailbag: How can they fix their abysmal ABS success rate?

around_teams.title: Is Marcell Ozuna Turning the Corner?
around_teams.latest_developments: A Post-Gazette piece June 25 assessed whether designated hitter Marcell Ozuna is breaking out of the slump that opened his Pirates tenure.
around_teams.summary: Marcell Ozuna started lousily after joining the Pirates, and the Post-Gazette weighed signs he may finally be hitting his stride under manager Don Kelly.

sources.title: Marcell Ozuna got off to a lousy start with the Pirates. Might he finally be turning the corner?

team_usa.title: U.S. Soccer Offers Pochettino a Deal Through 2030
team_usa.latest_developments: Multiple sources told the Guardian June 26 that U.S. Soccer has formally offered Mauricio Pochettino an extension through the 2030 World Cup, moving past the earlier positive talks.
team_usa.summary: U.S. Soccer has offered men's national team coach Mauricio Pochettino, in charge since late 2024, a contract extension that would keep him through the 2030 World Cup, even as European clubs are likely to pursue him.

sources.title: US Soccer offers contract renewal to Mauricio Pochettino through 2030 World Cup

team_usa.title: Pulisic Ready for the Knockout Round
team_usa.latest_developments: ESPN reported June 26 that Christian Pulisic, back from a calf injury, looks ready to make his mark as the United States heads into the World Cup round of 32.
team_usa.summary: Christian Pulisic returned in the 3-2 Group D finale loss to Türkiye after recovering from a left calf injury, and the United States, already through as group winners, plays its round-of-32 match July 1.

sources.title: Pulisic looks ready to make his mark on USMNT's World Cup

team_usa.title: Drug Case Against Bode Miller to Be Dropped
team_usa.latest_developments: An Idaho prosecutor said June 26 that misdemeanor drug charges against Olympic gold-medal skier Bode Miller will be dismissed.
team_usa.summary: Prosecutors will drop misdemeanor drug charges against former Olympic champion skier Bode Miller, despite an earlier finding of probable cause for his arrest, an Idaho prosecutor said.

sources.title: Drug case vs. ex-Olympian Miller to be dropped

reading.author: Ed Zitron
reading.title: Cargo Culture
reading.summary: Zitron argues that much of the tech and AI industry runs on cargo-cult thinking, imitating the surface rituals and rhetoric of past successes while lacking the substance that would make the products actually deliver.

reading.author: Stratechery
reading.title: An Interview with Figma CEO Dylan Field About Design and AI
reading.summary: Ben Thompson interviews Figma chief executive Dylan Field about building the design tool and why Field sees AI as a tailwind that strengthens, rather than threatens, Figma's position.

reading.author: Cal Newport
reading.title: Dear AI Companies: Stop the “Doom Trolling”
reading.summary: Newport urges AI companies to stop publishing alarming warnings about their own products' supposed dangers as a marketing move, likening it to a carmaker hyping fears about its own best-selling vehicle to signal how powerful it is.

sports_media: Not Just Football with Cam Heyward
